posts.text:
Most companies fail at blended workforces for one simple reason:
They measure humans one way and AI another. ⚠️

Customers don’t experience channels.
They experience outcomes.

High-performing teams measure experience consistency, not AI containment or agent utilization.

What actually matters 👇

🔵 Consistency beats utilization
If AI says one thing and a human says another, brand trust instantly erodes.

🔁 Escalations aren’t the problem
Bad handoffs are.
Context, timing, and skill match matter more than rate.

⚡ Learning velocity wins
How fast humans ramp.
How fast AI improves.
Speed to proficiency > automation %.

🚀 Change once. Deploy everywhere.
If humans and AI update differently, you risk chaos.

🧠 Customer Effort tells the truth
Repetition = friction, even when issues get resolved.

👉 The future isn’t humans vs. AI.
It’s humans and AI operating as one team.

🔗 Link in comments.

comments.text: Here’s a deeper breakdown of how to measure humans + AI as one operating system → https://bit.ly/4qU3Dro

Great insight Brian - We're always going to fixate on lagging indicators; however, the systems we create are far more predictable - The patterns that AI can produce and reproduce forge the behaviors driving the outcomes clients want. It's intelligent programming, now prompting that'll feed the collaboration with AI.
Gerard Baglieri Yes, I agree.

The brand consistency and trust is such a critical component here. I saw a stat recently that said it takes 12 positive customers experiences to negate just ONE bad experience. It's just so important to make sure the human-AI consistency, handoff, and change management are seamless.
Exactly Katie Leahy. Brands are built on repetition, not moments. If humans and AI don’t behave consistently, trust erodes fast—no matter how advanced the tech is.

This lands especially hard in practice.Most teams don’t lose trust because of AI mistakes they lose it when the handoff between AI and humans breaks consistency.Measuring them separately hides the real problem: ownership of the transition.Customers don’t care who handled the step. They feel whether the flow held.

I imagine that if the human agents knew they were measured differently, this would cause even more concerns about introducing AI Agents into the workforce. Consistency in training, analytics, and feedback is paramount to an organization's success.

posts.text:
As we move into a season that naturally invites reflection, we're reminded how grateful we are for the partners and leaders driving the future of CX and frontline performance.

Last week in Dallas, we teamed up with ibex to host an invite-only CX + AI Innovation Workshop, bringing together 20+ senior leaders to explore where AI is making the biggest impact today and where it needs to go next.

Across the room, leaders leaned into some of the most important questions facing frontline teams:
• How AI can accelerate onboarding and upskilling
• Where AI supports associates during live interactions
• What "good" looks like for adoption, performance, and customer experience
• How to bring human and AI agents together as one team

The conversations were candid, energetic, and incredibly forward-thinking, from breakout discussions to hands-on exploration of how Simulation, Performance Analysis, and AI Agents work together.

We closed the evening with a private dinner that sparked even more connection and idea sharing—and yes, unbelievable food.

A sincere thank you to our presenters Casey Denby and Pavel Sardar, the leaders who joined us for an engaging and thoughtful session, and our broader community who continues to push the industry forward.

More city events are coming soon; we cannot wait to keep building what is possible together!

#CustomerExperience #AIInnovation #AIinCX

posts.text:
Most contact centers are investing more in training than ever. Performance still isn't moving.

It's not a people problem. It's a system problem.

Yesterday at CCW Executive Exchange in Austin, our VP of Account Management, Liz Powell, took the stage to break down why and what actually works.

Our take: training, QA, and coaching don't fail because the tools are bad or the people aren't trying. They fail because they're disconnected. Feedback is delayed. Learning is separated from live work. And when AI gets layered onto that fragmented system, you don't fix the problem, you just get more dashboards of the same broken outcomes.

The organizations seeing real performance gains aren't adding more to the stack. They're rethinking the system entirely and building tight feedback loops where practice, live performance, and coaching continuously inform each other.

Because practice doesn't make perfect.
Feedback loops do.

Thank you to the CCW Executive Exchange for the opportunity to be in the room with so many sharp CX leaders. 🙌

posts.text: Agent training should not be checking boxes or static moments in time.  Check out our blog on simulation based training as a way to stay current, address real-time issues and to use automation as a way to challenge agents and keep people on their toes!  Zenarate is leading the market in this space and demonstrating significant returns to organizations using their tech.

Its entirely possible that L&D will absorb everything AI has to offer—faster content, personalized delivery, simulation at scale—and still find a way to avoid the one question that actually matters:

Did it work?

We've been wrestling with this for quite some time at Zenarate, building ROI dashboards, skills correlation capabilities, and more. These are critical - but also inherently retrospective.

What's been missing in the industry is a way to predict whether the programs we're designing have a realistic chance of producing durable skill transfer.

We're addressing it with Zenarate's new Skills Index. Powered by insights from 15M+ simulations and AI coaching sessions, it's a quite possibly a world-first. Learn more in my latest blog post. 

https://lnkd.in/ejbeDiQQ

Huge thanks to the DATAMARK, Inc. team for the partnership and for raising the bar on frontline readiness.

When agents get to practice real customer moments before they’re live, everyone wins:
- Faster ramp
- Fewer costly mistakes
- More confident, consistent CX

This is what it looks like when training is built for the moments that matter.

#AgentTraining #CustomerExperience #BPO #FrontlinePerformance #Zenarate

posts.text:
Thank you to Claude J. Werder Werder and the Brandon Hall Group team for a thoughtful and insightful briefing. This write-up captures why so many organizations are rethinking how they train, coach, and measure frontline performance.

Claude highlighted a shift we see every day in contact centers and other customer-facing teams. Scripts and classroom models no longer prepare agents for the open-ended conversations customers expect. The focus on practice that mirrors real interactions is why enterprise organizations adopt our AI Simulation and Coaching approach.

He also underscored the need to connect realistic practice with measurable performance. That's the foundation of Zenarate Analyze, which gives leaders clear insight into quality, consistency, and improvement across their teams.

We appreciate Claude's perspective on where the industry is heading and the role Zenarate is playing in shaping the new standard for frontline performance.

More to come as we continue advancing how human and AI agents learn, perform, and improve together.

posts.text:
AI simulation training isn't the future of customer service, it's happening right now at leading airlines like Spirit.

Airlines have been struggling with the same training problems for years. Most are still stuck with outdated agent-to-agent role plays, but Spirit Airlines completely changed their approach with AI-powered conversation and software simulations. The results? 

→ Revenue per call grew across both voice and chat agents 
→ Faster time to proficiency during nesting periods
→ Agents showed up on the floor more confident and better prepared 
→ Training inconsistencies were eliminated across all contact centers

This wasn't just a training upgrade. It was a reimagining.

Now, agents can have realistic conversations with AI that feel like actual Guest interactions and go on the floor more confident and ready to crush Guest expectations.

Curious how they pulled this off?

The full Spirit Airlines case study breaks it all down: https://lnkd.in/ePaR4iJj

posts.text:
The 2025 McKinsey State of AI report confirms themes we've seen in sessions with our Fortune 500 customers in recent months...

1. Market leaders expect true transformation (not incremental improvements) from their AI initiatives
2. Success often requires re-designing workflows, not just automating them
3. But a huge number of organizations are barely beyond pilot mode
4. And only 10% have scaled AI Agents 

What surprises me is how many leaders still tell me they feel 'behind.' 

The noise from all our LinkedIn feeds implies everyone else has 'figured it out.' They haven't. It's pure FOMO. 

When we advise customers starting AI agent projects with us to stop and plan for things like business case design, cluster analysis, data quality, governance, and talent readiness, there's a look of relief. 

Yes - it's the AI era. But yes - common sense still applies. 

https://lnkd.in/e63xK8MQ

Behind every top-performing team is a culture of learning that actually works.

Our VP of Global Sales, Luc Dallaire, recently joined Vic Pepe on the Ten Thousand Miles Podcast to share how companies can reimagine training—using AI, feedback, and real-world simulations to turn learning into lasting behavior change.

They dive into:
- Why many corporate training models are failing
- What neuroscience tells us about how people really learn
- The cultural shifts that make training stick

Tune in to hear how forward-thinking leaders are transforming development and performance: https://lnkd.in/ggf6x8GC

#Learning #Leadership #AI #CorporateTraining #FutureOfWork

We explored:
• Why leaders are increasingly dissatisfied with outdated training models.
• How active practice, feedback, and memory shape real skill development.
• The cultural shifts required for training to actually stick.
• How personalization and technology can reshape learning at scale.
• The future of corporate training - where simulations and practical experience take center stage.

This conversation isn’t just for learning leaders, it’s for anyone who believes investing in people is the most powerful strategy a company can embrace.

The Outlier Project D&M Enterprise Group

#TenThousandMiles #Leadership #Learning #Neuroscience #CorporateTraining #Innovation #FutureOfWork #EmployeeExperience #LearningCulture #VicPepe

We’re beyond excited to announce Michael McCourt (a former astrophysicist!) as a speaker for our upcoming Innovation Summit.

Mike brings a rare perspective. An astrophysicist turned data scientist and founder, he’s spent years studying how complex systems actually behave, and where our models fall short. 

In his session, he’ll dive into something we don’t talk about enough:
Why the tools we rely on to interpret performance, including LLMs, aren’t always the best way to understand what’s really driving outcomes.

Because knowing WHAT happened isn’t the same as understanding WHY it happened.

If you’re thinking about the future of AI and performance, this is one you won’t want to miss.

posts.text:
Last year we told you to Expect Fireworks. This year, it's time to Ignite Performance.
 
Join us again this year as we gather customer experience leaders at the 2026 Zenarate Innovation Summit to tackle a pressing question: As AI continues to transform the definition of frontline, how do you bring human learning and performance coaching + AI agents together under one unified strategy?
 
Hosted at Disney's Swan Reserve Resort on May 7–8, this isn't a product demo dressed up as an event. It's a day and a half of honest, forward-looking conversation about what's actually working on the front line — and what isn't. The agenda includes:
 
→ Ignite Performance: Practical steps to connect AI-powered simulations, personalized learning, and performance data in a truly integrated ecosystem 
→ Ignite Customer Experience: A participant-led debate on the pros, cons, and future of modern AutoQA and CX Insights 
→ Ignite Data: Why LLMs are often the wrong tool for understanding what's driving frontline performance — and what to use instead 
→ Ignite Agents: A candid look at lessons learned from 100+ AI agent deployments, and what it means for the future of hybrid human + AI teams working as one
 
And because no trip to Orlando should be wasted, we've reserved a private terrace inside Epcot's World Showcase so our guests can close the Summit with Disney fireworks. Park ticket included! 🎆
 
Register below to join fellow leaders from Verizon, Chase, Nintendo, Hyatt, and many more as we work to shape the future of frontline performance.

Link to register: https://lnkd.in/eT_U4W3c

These conversations are exactly why we love bringing leaders together to share what’s actually working, along with the performance challenges they’re solving every day.

posts.text:
🔥 Knowledge checks ≠ real-world performance.

That’s one of the biggest truths contact center leaders are waking up to—and it’s reshaping how teams are trained and coached in the AI era.

Here are 3 takeaways from our latest blog on modernizing service experiences with AI:

1️⃣ Assessments don’t equal mastery. 80% of agents who aced quizzes couldn’t apply the skill in live scenarios. Simulation training closes that gap by measuring tone, judgment, and compliance under pressure.

2️⃣ De-escalation must be a core skill. With 68% of customers demanding escalation, agents need practice handling frustrated, time-pressed callers. AI-driven role plays give them the confidence to navigate tough moments.

3️⃣ Next-gen agents need next-gen coaching. Leaders are prioritizing AI fluency, decision-making, and empathy. Simulation data provides the skills profile and readiness signals needed to coach intentionally.

👉 For more insights—and action steps you can apply right now—read the full blog: [link in comments]

#ContactCenter #AI #CustomerExperience #Coaching #DigitalTransformation

Imagine if the customer experience could work seamlessly between human and AI.
